questions.code:
console.log(add(2, 3));
function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
questions.explanation: Function DECLARATIONS are hoisted — you can call them before their definition.
JS is loose with extras. Modern way to capture: rest params (...args).
Default value 'friend' fires when the arg is undefined or missing.
Execution stops at the first return.

questions.prompt: A function is a value. Which is TRUE?
options: Functions can be stored in variables and passed around
Functions cannot be arguments to other functions
Functions can only be declared, never assigned
questions.explanation: First-class functions — the core of JS.

questions.prompt: Which is the SHORTEST correct arrow that doubles n?
questions.explanation: No braces = implicit return. Braces need explicit return.

questions.code:
const add = (a, b) => a + b;
console.log(add(1, 2, 3));
questions.explanation: Only the first two args are used. Third is silently ignored.
